# API Reference

Complete reference for the Lighthouse Attention Lab REST API.

**Base URL:** `http://localhost:8000`  
**Interactive docs:** `http://localhost:8000/docs` (Swagger UI, auto-generated by FastAPI)  
**OpenAPI schema:** `http://localhost:8000/openapi.json`

---

## Table of Contents

- [Overview](#overview)
- [GET /api/health](#get-apihealth)
- [GET /api/examples](#get-apiexamples)
- [POST /api/analyze](#post-apianalyze)
- [POST /api/benchmark](#post-apibenchmark)
- [Error Responses](#error-responses)
- [CORS](#cors)
- [Field Reference](#field-reference)

---

## Overview

| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/api/health` | GET | Service liveness check |
| `/api/examples` | GET | Retrieve built-in example documents |
| `/api/analyze` | POST | Analyse a document: tokens, masks, edges, memory, retrieval |
| `/api/benchmark` | POST | Compute scaling curves for a range of sequence lengths |

All request and response bodies are JSON. The `Content-Type` header must be `application/json` for POST requests.

---

## GET /api/health

Returns the service status. Use this to confirm the backend is running before sending analysis requests.

### Request

```
GET /api/health
```

No parameters.

### Response — 200 OK

```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "service": "lighthouse-attention-lab"
}
```

### Example

```bash
curl http://localhost:8000/api/health
```

---

## GET /api/examples

Returns the three built-in long-document examples. The frontend calls this on page load to populate the example picker.

### Request

```
GET /api/examples
```

No parameters.

### Response — 200 OK

Array of `ExampleDocument` objects:

```json
[
  {
    "id": "incident-cache-root-cause",
    "title": "Incident report: hidden root cause",
    "description": "A service incident where the key root cause is documented early, then buried under a long operational timeline.",
    "document": "Opening investigation note. The checkout incident was traced to...",
    "query": "Which stale feature flag cache TTL caused the issue, and what should it change to?",
    "hidden_fact_position_hint": "The root cause appears in the opening investigation notes."
  },
  {
    "id": "manual-warranty-rule",
    "title": "Product manual: warranty rule in the middle",
    "description": "...",
    "document": "...",
    "query": "...",
    "hidden_fact_position_hint": "..."
  },
  {
    "id": "meeting-notes-key-decision",
    "title": "Meeting notes: buried launch decision",
    "description": "...",
    "document": "...",
    "query": "...",
    "hidden_fact_position_hint": "..."
  }
]
```

### Example

```bash
curl http://localhost:8000/api/examples | python -m json.tool
```

---

## POST /api/analyze

The core endpoint. Tokenises the document, computes attention edge counts, builds attention pattern matrices, creates lighthouse chunks with summaries, and runs a retrieval simulation.

### Request Body

```json
{
  "document": "string (required, 1–50 000 chars)",
  "query": "string (required, 1–2 000 chars)",
  "window_size": 32,
  "chunk_size": 128,
  "embedding_dim": 64,
  "visualization_token_cap": 160
}
```

| Field | Type | Default | Range | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `document` | string | — | 1–50,000 chars | The document to analyse |
| `query` | string | — | 1–2,000 chars | The query token (treated as appearing at the end of the document) |
| `window_size` | integer | 32 | 1–512 | Radius W of the local attention window |
| `chunk_size` | integer | 128 | 8–1024 | Number of tokens per lighthouse chunk |
| `embedding_dim` | integer | 64 | 8–512 | Dimensionality of the deterministic hash embeddings |
| `visualization_token_cap` | integer | 160 | 32–256 | Maximum normal-token rows/columns in the returned heatmap matrices |

### Response Body — 200 OK

```json
{
  "token_count": 312,
  "chunk_count": 3,
  "lighthouse_token_count": 3,

  "edge_counts": {
    "full": 97344,
    "sliding_window": 20088,
    "lighthouse": 21039
  },

  "reductions": {
    "sliding_vs_full_percent": 79.36,
    "lighthouse_vs_full_percent": 78.38
  },

  "memory_estimates": {
    "full_attention_mb_fp16": 0.1858,
    "sliding_attention_mb_fp16": 0.0384,
    "lighthouse_attention_mb_fp16": 0.0402,
    "full_attention_mb_fp32": 0.3716,
    "sliding_attention_mb_fp32": 0.0768,
    "lighthouse_attention_mb_fp32": 0.0803
  },

  "attention_patterns": {
    "visual_token_count": 160,
    "visual_lighthouse_count": 2,
    "full": [[1,1,1,...], ...],
    "sliding": [[1,1,0,...], ...],
    "lighthouse": [[1,1,0,...,2,2], ...],
    "legend": {
      "0": "blocked",
      "1": "local attention",
      "2": "lighthouse summary access",
      "3": "lighthouse reads own chunk",
      "4": "lighthouse-to-lighthouse"
    }
  },

  "chunks": [
    {
      "chunk_id": 0,
      "start_token": 0,
      "end_token": 127,
      "top_keywords": ["cache", "ttl", "checkout", "stale", "workers", "rule"],
      "summary": "The checkout incident was traced to a stale feature flag cache with a forty eight hour TTL."
    }
  ],

  "retrieval": {
    "query": "Which stale feature flag cache TTL caused the issue?",
    "full_attention": {
      "top_token_index": 14,
      "score": 0.6821,
      "snippet": "traced to a stale feature flag cache with a forty eight hour TTL the rollout controller"
    },
    "sliding_window": {
      "can_reach_best_token": false,
      "best_reachable_score": 0.4102,
      "snippet": "action review will happen in thirty days owners will bring evidence"
    },
    "lighthouse_attention": {
      "top_chunk_id": 0,
      "score": 0.5944,
      "summary": "The checkout incident was traced to a stale feature flag cache with a forty eight hour TTL.",
      "top_keywords": ["cache", "ttl", "checkout", "stale", "workers", "rule"]
    },
    "plain_english_takeaway": "Sliding-window attention cannot directly reach the useful evidence because it is outside the local window. Lighthouse-style attention can still route the query toward the relevant chunk summary."
  },

  "runtime_estimates": {
    "edge_count_ratio_lighthouse_to_full": 0.216134,
    "edge_count_ratio_sliding_to_full": 0.206382
  }
}
```

### Field Descriptions

#### `edge_counts`

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `full` | N² — all token pairs |
| `sliding_window` | Exact count using O(1) formula with boundary handling |
| `lighthouse` | sliding + N×L + N + L² where L = chunk count |

#### `reductions`

Percentage reduction from full attention: `(1 − method/full) × 100`.

#### `memory_estimates`

Memory in megabytes assuming 2 bytes/weight (fp16) or 4 bytes/weight (fp32).

#### `attention_patterns`

Integer-coded matrices. See [Field Reference → Attention Codes](#attention-pattern-codes).

The `full` and `sliding` matrices are `visual_token_count × visual_token_count`.  
The `lighthouse` matrix is `(visual_token_count + visual_lighthouse_count) × (visual_token_count + visual_lighthouse_count)`.

#### `retrieval`

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `full_attention.top_token_index` | Token position (0-indexed) of the highest-similarity span in the document |
| `full_attention.score` | Cosine similarity mapped to [0, 1] |
| `full_attention.snippet` | ±18 tokens around `top_token_index` |
| `sliding_window.can_reach_best_token` | Whether `top_token_index` falls within the final W tokens |
| `sliding_window.best_reachable_score` | Best score achievable within the local window |
| `lighthouse_attention.top_chunk_id` | Chunk with highest similarity to the query (0-indexed) |
| `lighthouse_attention.score` | Similarity score of the best chunk's summary + keywords vector |
| `plain_english_takeaway` | One or two sentences explaining whether sliding-window succeeded or failed |

#### `runtime_estimates`

Ratios of edge counts. A ratio of 0.07 means the method uses 7% as many connections as full attention. **These are not measured GPU timings.** Actual GPU speedup depends on hardware and kernel implementation.

### Example

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/analyze \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "document": "Root cause: stale feature flag cache TTL was forty eight hours. The checkout team found the error after reviewing worker logs.",
    "query": "What caused the incident?",
    "window_size": 8,
    "chunk_size": 32,
    "embedding_dim": 32,
    "visualization_token_cap": 64
  }'
```

### Python example

```python
import httpx

response = httpx.post(
    "http://localhost:8000/api/analyze",
    json={
        "document": "Root cause: stale feature flag cache TTL was forty eight hours. ...",
        "query": "What caused the incident?",
        "window_size": 32,
        "chunk_size": 128,
        "embedding_dim": 64,
        "visualization_token_cap": 160,
    },
)
data = response.json()
print(f"Tokens: {data['token_count']}")
print(f"Full edges: {data['edge_counts']['full']}")
print(f"Lighthouse reduction: {data['reductions']['lighthouse_vs_full_percent']:.1f}%")
print(f"Can sliding reach best token: {data['retrieval']['sliding_window']['can_reach_best_token']}")
```

---

## POST /api/benchmark

Computes edge counts, reductions, memory estimates, and runtime ratios for a list of sequence lengths. No attention matrices are allocated — only the O(1) formula is used.

### Request Body

```json
{
  "lengths": [256, 512, 1024, 2048, 4096],
  "window_size": 32,
  "chunk_size": 128
}
```

| Field | Type | Default | Range | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `lengths` | integer[] | [256, 512, 1024, 2048, 4096] | 1–12 positive integers, each ≤ 65,536 | Sequence lengths to benchmark. Sorted, deduplicated, and capped internally. |
| `window_size` | integer | 32 | 1–512 | Window radius for sliding/lighthouse calculations |
| `chunk_size` | integer | 128 | 8–4096 | Chunk size for lighthouse calculations |

### Response Body — 200 OK

```json
{
  "points": [
    {
      "length": 256,
      "edge_counts": {
        "full": 65536,
        "sliding_window": 16640,
        "lighthouse": 17408
      },
      "reductions": {
        "sliding_vs_full_percent": 74.61,
        "lighthouse_vs_full_percent": 73.44
      },
      "memory_estimates": {
        "full_attention_mb_fp16": 0.125,
        "sliding_attention_mb_fp16": 0.0317,
        "lighthouse_attention_mb_fp16": 0.0332,
        "full_attention_mb_fp32": 0.25,
        "sliding_attention_mb_fp32": 0.0635,
        "lighthouse_attention_mb_fp32": 0.0664
      },
      "safe_runtime_estimates_ms": {
        "full": 0.044,
        "sliding_window": 0.011,
        "lighthouse": 0.012
      }
    },
    { "length": 512, "...": "..." },
    { "length": 1024, "...": "..." },
    { "length": 2048, "...": "..." },
    { "length": 4096, "...": "..." }
  ]
}
```

> `safe_runtime_estimates_ms` is computed as `edges / 1_500_000` — a proportional estimate only. It is not measured GPU timing.

### Example

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/benchmark \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "lengths": [512, 1024, 2048, 4096, 8192],
    "window_size": 32,
    "chunk_size": 128
  }'
```

---

## Error Responses

### 422 Unprocessable Entity

Returned when the request body fails Pydantic validation: missing required field, value out of range, wrong type, or string exceeding its maximum length.

Common triggers:

| Condition | Example |
|---|---|
| Missing required field | `document` or `query` omitted |
| `document` longer than 50,000 chars | Very large paste |
| `query` longer than 2,000 chars | Oversized query |
| `lengths` list longer than 12 items | Too many benchmark points |
| `window_size` outside 1–512 | `window_size: 0` |

```json
{
  "detail": [
    {
      "type": "missing",
      "loc": ["body", "document"],
      "msg": "Field required",
      "input": {}
    }
  ]
}
```

### 400 Bad Request

Returned when the request is valid JSON but the business logic rejects it (e.g. document produces zero tokens after tokenisation).

```json
{
  "detail": "document must contain at least one token."
}
```

### 500 Internal Server Error

Unexpected errors. If you see one, please open an issue with the request body and the full error message.

---

## CORS

The API allows all origins with no credential requirements:

```python
allow_origins=["*"]
allow_methods=["*"]
allow_headers=["*"]
```

This means you can call the API directly from any browser, Postman, curl, or frontend running on any port without CORS errors.

---

## Field Reference

### Attention Pattern Codes

| Integer code | Colour in UI | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `0` | Dark / blocked | No connection allowed |
| `1` | Blue | Local or full attention allowed |
| `2` | Gold | Normal token attending to a lighthouse token |
| `3` | Green | Lighthouse token reading its own chunk |
| `4` | Pink | Lighthouse token attending to another lighthouse token |

### Similarity Score Range

All similarity scores are cosine similarity mapped to [0, 1]:

```
score = (cosine_similarity + 1) / 2
```

A score of 1.0 means identical vectors. A score of 0.5 means orthogonal (unrelated) vectors. A score near 0.0 means opposing vectors.

Because the vectors are deterministic hash-based (not semantic), scores above ~0.7 indicate genuine keyword overlap between the query and the span/chunk.
